root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide
root.summary: Overview of the Cúram 8.1.2 Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide. This front matter and first chapter introduce the MDT portal as a collaboration tool for managing cases, clients, meetings, tasks, discussions, and team contact information. It covers the home page and profile, including links to cases, clients, calendar, tasks, teams, online profile updates, and incident reporting, plus summary widgets and admin-managed login/security details. It explains collaborative case types and common functions such as discussions, meeting scheduling, minutes, notes, attachments, and multidisciplinary team membership. The guide also describes discussion participation roles, calendar use with PIM/email integration for meeting invites, meeting response statuses, recording and issuing meeting minutes, and task inbox/history handling. The remaining pages are notices and legal terms, including usage restrictions, warranty disclaimer, privacy policy, and trademarks.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Introductory notice for the document. It instructs readers to review the “Notices” section on page 17 before using the information or the supported product, and includes the Merative US L.P. copyright statement (2012, 2024).

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Edition note for the Cúram Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide. This section states the edition applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2, and includes copyright/trademark notices plus the table of contents. It previews the guide’s main topics: home page/profile, cases and clients, incident reporting, multidisciplinary teams, discussion participation, calendar/meeting scheduling, and tasks.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-16
children.summary: Describes meeting minutes and task handling in the Cúram Multidisciplinary Team Portal. Meeting invites can be marked tentative. The meeting minutes wizard captures details, notes/decisions, attendance, actions, files, related cases, and a summary, with work-in-progress support; minutes are issued by email as a PDF. Also covers the “Your Tasks” inbox: viewing assigned tasks, adding comments, closing tasks, linking to related meeting minutes, and viewing task history with action and timestamp records.

Edition note for the Cúram Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide. Applies to Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2, with copyright/trademark notices and the table of contents. The section previews portal features: home page/profile, cases and clients, incident reporting, multidisciplinary teams, discussion participation, calendar and meeting scheduling, meeting minutes, and tasks. It also covers meeting invite responses, recording and issuing minutes, and task inbox/history handling.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: Contains the document’s legal notices: a Merative copyright/license statement for sample programs, including permission to copy/modify/distribute them with no warranty and no liability. Also points to Merative’s privacy policy URL and lists trademark acknowledgments for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX. Useful for finding licensing terms, privacy policy access, or trademark information.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: Lists trademark and copyright notices for Merative documentation. Notes that sample names are fictitious, sample programs are provided “AS IS” under the Merative Client Agreement, and links to Merative’s privacy policy. Identifies trademarks for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle/Java, Linux, Microsoft/Windows, and UNIX, and states other names may be trademarks of others. Includes copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

Legal notices for the publication: terms for personal and enterprise use, requiring preservation of proprietary notices and prohibiting derivative works or redistribution outside an enterprise without consent. States Merative grants no additional rights, may withdraw permission, and requires compliance with export laws. Includes an “AS-IS” no-warranty disclaimer, patent notice, third-party product/site disclaimer, and copyright © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024. Useful for licensing, usage restrictions, and warranty/legal terms.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: Table of contents for the Cúram Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide. Lists major sections on home page and profile, cases and clients, incident reporting, multidisciplinary teams, discussion participation, calendar and meeting scheduling/minutes, and tasks/work management. Also includes front matter links for note, edition, notices, privacy policy, and trademarks, with page numbers for navigation.

children.title: 1 Cúram Multidisciplinary Team Portal Guide
1.1 Your Home Page and Profile
Your Home Page
children.summary: This section describes the portal home page and profile features. It explains the menu links to cases, clients, calendar, tasks, teams, and the user profile, plus a quick link to report an incident. It also notes home-page summaries for meetings, tasks, meeting minutes, incidents, and watched discussions. The profile area covers updating contact details and viewing team information, while username, password, and security level must be maintained by a system administrator.

children.title: Your Profile
children.summary: This section explains the “Your Profile” area of the Cúram Multidisciplinary Team Portal. It covers profile contact details, a summary of your multidisciplinary teams, and links to update your name, role, phone numbers, mailing address, and email. It notes that profile updates are published across the organization, and that you can view team and lead member contact information. Username, password, and sensitivity security level are managed by a system administrator.

Explains the portal home page and profile area. The home page links to cases, clients, calendar, tasks, teams, and your online profile, plus a quick incident-report link, and shows summaries for meetings, tasks, meeting minutes, incidents, and watched discussions. The profile section lets you update contact details such as name, role, phones, address, and email, and view team and lead-member contacts. Username, password, and security level are managed by a system administrator.

children.title: 1.2 Your Cases and Clients
Your Cases
Common Collaborative Case Information
Discussions
children.summary: This section explains common collaborative case features in Cúram 8.1.2. It notes that multidisciplinary team members can access “My Cases” and view case types such as outcome plans, investigations, and social enterprise folders. The “Discussions” subsection says discussion boards let team members share advice and important information, with optional email notifications for new posts and comments. It also introduces related topics: meeting scheduling, recording meeting minutes, attachments, and multidisciplinary team membership.

children.title: Scheduling Meetings and Recording Meeting Minutes
children.summary: Explains that meetings can be scheduled from the calendar, with PIM integration enabling meeting invites to be accepted in personal calendars. Also describes the record meeting minutes wizard, which captures meeting details, notes, decisions, attendees, actions, attachments, and links minutes to a case. Minutes can be automatically issued to invited participants. Useful if you need guidance on scheduling meetings or creating and distributing meeting minutes in collaborative cases.

children.title: Multidisciplinary Team
children.summary: Explains the My Cases tab in the multidisciplinary team portal and how it lists accessible collaborative outcome plans, investigation cases, and social enterprise folders. Describes case highlighting for recent updates and how the case home page differs by case type. Also covers common collaborative case information: discussion boards, meeting scheduling and minutes, attachments, and the multidisciplinary team context for collaboration.

children.title: Recording Notes
children.summary: Describes note recording in collaborative cases: notes can be saved with full history tracking, including who last updated them, when, and what changed. Also covers attaching supplemental files to collaborative cases and to meeting minutes, discussion comments, and incidents, with access controlled by user security privileges.

children.title: Attaching Relevant Files
children.summary: Describes attaching supplemental files to collaborative cases. Files can be added to cases and accessed by users with appropriate security privileges, and can also be attached to meeting minutes, discussion comments, and incidents. This section is useful if you need to manage or review related documents within collaborative case records.

Overview of common collaborative case information in Cúram 8.1.2. Explains that the My Cases tab lists collaborative outcome plans, investigations, and social enterprise folders accessible to multidisciplinary team members, with recent updates highlighted and case home pages varying by case type. Also covers shared collaboration features: discussion boards for advice and notifications, scheduling meetings from the calendar with PIM integration, recording and issuing meeting minutes, attachments, and the multidisciplinary team context.

children.title: Collaborative Outcome Plan Case Information
Activities
children.summary: This section defines activities in a collaborative outcome plan as scheduled services, referrals, or actions for a client. It explains that activity information helps give a fuller picture of the client’s current involvement and can identify other helpful activities.

children.title: Objectives
children.summary: Objectives are small, incremental steps within a collaborative outcome plan that help a client reach an ultimate goal. The section explains that objective information gives a clearer picture of what the client is trying to achieve, with an example such as remaining clean and sober. Use this node for understanding how objectives fit into case planning and goal tracking.

This section briefly describes additional information maintained for a collaborative outcome plan case. It covers activities, defined as scheduled services, referrals, or actions for a client, and explains that they help show current involvement and identify other helpful services. It also covers objectives, described as small steps toward an ultimate goal, helping clarify what the client is trying to achieve. Open this node if you need details on outcome plan case tracking, client activities, or goal/objective structure.

children.title: Collaborative Investigation Case Information
children.summary: This section explains that collaborative investigation case information shows the number of open and decided allegations to help assess investigation seriousness. It also introduces related collaborative social enterprise folder content: incidents, cases, and client lists. It notes that incident reporting supports prevention and client safety, can trigger MDT email notifications, and includes details such as incident description, timing, affected clients, reporter, associated persons, and supporting documents.

children.title: Collaborative Social Enterprise Folder Case Information
Incidents
children.summary: Explains why incident reporting is important in collaborative social enterprise cases: it helps track family history, prevent escalation, and support timely intervention. Notes that multidisciplinary team members can be emailed automatically when incidents affecting their clients are reported, updated, or closed. Also introduces incident reporting for affected clients, including the details captured: description, date/time, affected clients, reporter, associated persons and roles, and supporting files/evidence.

children.title: Cases
children.summary: Explains how collaborative social enterprise folders organize clients and their associated cases to give a holistic view of agency interactions. Notes that collaborative investigation case information shows the number of open and decided allegations to gauge seriousness. Also mentions the client list in the MDT portal, with contact details and email links for clients in collaborative cases.

This section explains collaborative social enterprise folder case information. It covers incident reporting, including why incidents must be tracked to prevent escalation and enable timely family intervention, and that MDT members can be emailed when incidents affecting their clients are reported, updated, or closed. It also describes collaborative case organization: clients and related cases are grouped for a holistic agency view, collaborative investigation cases show open and decided allegations, and MDT users can view a combined client list with contact details and email links.

“My Cases” lists all collaborative social enterprise folders, outcome plans, and investigations you belong to in the MDT portal, highlighting cases with recent updates. The case home page differs by case type: social enterprise folders show clients and related cases, outcome plans show clients and objectives, and investigations show clients plus open/decided allegations. The section also covers shared case features: discussion boards with optional email alerts, calendar-based meeting scheduling with PIM integration, meeting minutes, notes/history, attachments, and MDT membership management.

children.title: Your Clients
children.summary: This section explains the “Your Clients” list in the multidisciplinary team portal. It shows the combined list of all clients tied to collaborative cases where the user is an MDT member, and provides each client’s contact details plus an email link if an address exists. The surrounding page also notes collaborative case information, incident reporting, and how incident updates can notify team members. Use this node for access to client lists, contact/email access, and related collaborative case context.

This section explains how the MDT portal’s My Cases and Your Clients areas provide access to all collaborative cases and client lists for a user. It covers case types (social enterprise folders, outcome plans, investigations), updated-case highlighting, and the different home page views for each type. It also summarizes common case functions: discussions, email alerts, calendar meeting scheduling with PIM integration, meeting minutes, notes/history, attachments, and MDT membership management.

children.title: 1.3 Reporting Incidents
Reporting an Incident for an Affected Client
children.summary: Explains how to report an incident for an affected client. Incidents are events that directly or potentially negatively affect a client’s health or safety, such as neglect, abuse, or workplace accidents. The section lists the information that can be entered: incident description and date/time, affected clients, reporter details, associated persons and roles, and supporting documentation/files. Useful for determining whether the node covers incident reporting requirements and what data can be captured.

children.title: Viewing Incidents for Your Clients
children.summary: Incidents reported for your clients appear automatically in all affected clients’ social enterprise folders. From this view, you can edit incident details, add participants, change the reporter, add another affected client, attach a file, or delete the incident.

Explains how to report incidents for affected clients in collaborative social enterprise folders. Incidents are events that directly or potentially negatively affect a client’s health or safety, such as neglect, abuse, or workplace accidents. The section lists the data that can be recorded: incident description, date/time, affected clients, reporter name, associated persons and their roles, and supporting documentation/files. Use this node to find incident reporting requirements and the available incident details that can be captured.

children.title: 1.4 Your Multidisciplinary Teams
children.summary: The Teams tab shows your multidisciplinary teams and lets you expand each team to view members. It provides contact options for team members, including emailing the whole team or individuals, scheduling a meeting with all members, and viewing each member’s phone number.

children.title: 1.5 Your Discussion Participation
Viewing Your Discussions
children.summary: Explains discussion boards in the MDT Portal: they support comments and questions on a topic to improve coordination among multidisciplinary team members and agency workers. Covers key roles—discussion moderator (typically the owner, responsible for managing and uniquely closing discussions) and discussion watchers (can view, contribute, and receive email notifications). Notes that users are usually auto-added as watchers if they are MDT members, the moderator, or the creator, and external users can start or stop watching discussions.

children.title: Creating New Discussions and Adding Discussion Comments
children.summary: Explains how to create a new discussion by entering a title and first comment, with optional file attachment. The discussion sensitivity defaults to level 1 and can only be raised up to the creator’s own sensitivity level; only users at or above that level may participate. The excerpt then begins the “Your Calendar” section, describing viewing invited team meetings, scheduling team meetings, recording minutes, managing attendees, and PIM/Outlook integration for accept/decline responses.

This section explains discussion boards in the MDT Portal: how they support comments and questions on a topic to improve coordination among multidisciplinary team members and agency workers. It defines the discussion moderator (usually the owner, who manages discussions and is the only one who can close them) and discussion watchers (can view, contribute, and get email notifications). It also notes default watcher assignment for MDT members, moderators, and creators, and that external users can stop or restart watching a discussion.

children.title: 1.6 Your Calendar
Viewing Scheduled Meetings in Your Calendar
children.summary: Explains how the calendar is used to view team meetings you’re invited to, open meeting details, see or record meeting minutes, and manage meetings you scheduled (invite/remove attendees, cancel). Also covers scheduling team meetings, automatic contact of team members, and PIM integration with external calendars like Outlook. Meeting responses can be accepted or declined; accepted invites add reminders to both Cúram and the PIM calendar, while declined invites remain in Cúram but not the PIM calendar.

children.title: Scheduling Meetings
children.summary: This section explains how to use the calendar to view invited team meetings and schedule new team meetings. It covers inviting the whole team, automatic contact of members with available details, and managing meetings you scheduled (attendees, cancellations, minutes). It also describes PIM integration with external calendars such as Microsoft Outlook, where meeting requests can appear in both Cúram and email calendars. Invitees with email can accept or decline without logging into Cúram, and their response updates the collaborative case status.

children.title: Recording Meeting Minutes Wizard
children.summary: Describes the Recording Meeting Minutes Wizard for capturing and issuing meeting minutes. Covers work-in-progress entry and key steps: meeting details, notes and decisions, attendance, actions with responsible person and due date, file attachments, linked collaborative cases, and a summary review. Minutes can be issued to attendees and other interested parties, typically by email with a PDF attachment, and missing/invalid email addresses trigger an informational message.

This section explains how to use Your Calendar to view invited multidisciplinary team meetings, open meeting details, see or record minutes, and manage meetings you scheduled by adding/removing attendees or canceling. It also covers scheduling team meetings, automatically contacting team members, and PIM integration with external calendars such as Outlook. Invitees with email can accept or decline without logging into Cúram; accepted invites add reminders to both Cúram and the PIM calendar, while declined invites stay only in Cúram.

children.title: 1.7 Your Tasks
Viewing Work Assigned to You
children.summary: This section explains the portal inbox for viewing work assigned to you. It says your multidisciplinary team portal lists tasks assigned to you, and each task describes an action you are responsible for completing. It also notes that you can add comments to tasks assigned to you. Useful for users who need to monitor and update their assigned work.

children.title: Managing Your Work
children.summary: Explains how to manage assigned tasks: add comments, close a task when completed or no longer relevant, and follow a link to related meeting minutes when the task comes from a scheduled meeting. Notes that each task has a history showing all lifecycle events, including who performed each action and when.

This section explains the “Your Tasks” inbox in the multidisciplinary team portal. It shows that assigned tasks appear in your inbox, describe actions you are responsible for, and can come from meeting minutes or other agency processes. It also covers task management: adding comments, closing tasks when complete or no longer relevant, viewing linked meeting minutes for meeting-related tasks, and using the task history to see all lifecycle events, including who did each action and when.

Overview of the MDT Portal guide. Covers the home page and profile (links to cases, clients, calendar, tasks, teams, online profile, incident reporting, and summary widgets; profile contact details can be updated but username/password/security level are admin-managed). Explains accessing collaborative cases and clients, case types and common features, incident reporting basics, team/member contact functions, discussion boards and watcher/moderator roles, calendar meeting scheduling with PIM integration and meeting minutes, and the tasks inbox with comments, closure, linked minutes, and task history.
